title: Homework 3
text:
Preamble
This assignment practices reading, combining, and manipulating different data types, such as Excel, comma-separated values (CSV), and databases.


Task 1: Conceptual Questions

If your working directory is myfolder/homework/, what relative path would you specify to get the file located at myfolder/MyData.csv?
I would use the command ../MyData.csv to access the file.
What are the m ajor benefits of using R projects?
It maintains a consistent working directory.
What is Git and what is GitHub?
Git is a code version manager and GitHub is an online Git center.
What are the two main differences between a tibble and a data.frame?
A tibble is “lazy and surly,”
Rewrite the following nested function call using baseR’s chaining operator:


library(tidyverse)
arrange(filter(select(as_tibble(iris), starts_with("Petal"), Species),
              Petal.Length < 1.55), Species)

# A tibble: 37 × 3
   Petal.Length Petal.Width Species
          <dbl>       <dbl> <fct>  
 1          1.4         0.2 setosa 
 2          1.4         0.2 setosa 
 3          1.3         0.2 setosa 
 4          1.5         0.2 setosa 
 5          1.4         0.2 setosa 
 6          1.4         0.3 setosa 
 7          1.5         0.2 setosa 
 8          1.4         0.2 setosa 
 9          1.5         0.1 setosa 
10          1.5         0.2 setosa 
# ℹ 27 more rows


The appropriate code using the chaining operator is

library(tidyverse)
as_tibble(iris) |>
  select(starts_with("Petal"), Species) |>
  filter(Petal.Length < 1.55) |>
  arrange(Species)

# A tibble: 37 × 3
   Petal.Length Petal.Width Species
          <dbl>       <dbl> <fct>  
 1          1.4         0.2 setosa 
 2          1.4         0.2 setosa 
 3          1.3         0.2 setosa 
 4          1.5         0.2 setosa 
 5          1.4         0.2 setosa 
 6          1.4         0.3 setosa 
 7          1.5         0.2 setosa 
 8          1.4         0.2 setosa 
 9          1.5         0.1 setosa 
10          1.5         0.2 setosa 
# ℹ 27 more rows



What is meant by long format data and wide format data? Which do we generally prefer for statistical analysis?
Wide format data is useful when a variable has several observations, so called because it tends to have fewer rows but more columns (one per observation) than the long format, which splits each of the observations across a different row, thus resulting in more rows but fewer columns. While the wide format is used for ease of viewing, for statistical analysis, the long format is preferred.
